reasoning: The Kansas City Chiefs opened the 2025 season with a surprising 0-2 start, leading to a crowded and competitive AFC West. However, their recent performance has them bouncing back to a 4-3 record (and currently heavy favorites heading into their Week 8 Monday Night Football game against the Commanders). Despite trailing the Denver Broncos (6-2) and being tied with the Los Angeles Chargers (5-3), who hold a weekly tiebreaker over them, the Chiefs' pedigree remains intact: they are ranked the #1 overall team in the NFL power rankings entering Week 8. The Polymarket price was 0.42 on October 16 when the Chiefs were 3-3; since then, they have logged a victory and find themselves on a winning streak. Although the Broncos and Chargers have both won out during this final October stretch, keeping the division tight, the Chiefs' status as a perennial powerhouse means they should retain strong odds to overcome a one-game deficit over the remaining 9 weeks of the season. I estimate the odds have held steady or marginally improved to 0.43.
